repair_candidate.summary: Repair and validate contributor PR #90468's prompt-template argument parser fix. Preserve prose apostrophes in contractions and possessives while retaining documented shell-style single and double quote grouping, including bare single-quote concatenation. Keep the patch scoped to the parser and regression tests, preserve @yetval authorship/credit, and do not merge or close anything in this run.
repair_candidate.pr_title: fix(agents): preserve apostrophes in prompt-template arguments
repair_candidate.pr_body:
## Summary

Repair the prompt-template slash-command argument parser so prose apostrophes remain literal without regressing documented shell-style quote behavior.

This carries forward @yetval's source PR #90468 and preserves attribution for the original apostrophe parsing fix.

## Scope

- keep contractions and prose apostrophes literal, including `don't`, `it's`, `users'`, `O'Brien's`, and `rock'n'roll`
- preserve shell-style single/double quoted spans and bare single-quote concatenation such as `foo'bar baz' next`
- prevent contractions or plural possessives from consuming later standalone quoted spans

## Validation

- `pnpm -s vitest run packages/agent-core/src/harness/prompt-templates.test.ts`
- `pnpm check:changed`

Source PR: https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/90468
Credited author: @yetval
credit_notes: Preserve contributor credit for @yetval as the source PR author.
Reference source PR https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/90468 in any replacement or repair PR body.
If user-facing release notes mention the parser fix, include attribution to @yetval/source PR #90468.
